segments.text: I sell white label media services to PR firms. We've been stuck at 1.5 million for five years. Agencies come to you.
Yeah. They then white label your services and sell your stuff to other people. That's exactly right.
Do you have the same number of agencies that are selling your stuff last year as this year and the year before that and the year before that?
It's pretty high churn for the most part.
Are they turning because they're not using the articles or because the articles are not good? Because they're not using it.
You probably need a lower tier that has some sort of usage component added on.
So it might be something like $1.99 a month plus 100 per article. You want to be low enough that they don't turn on their bad months. And so that's the
fundamental issues. I think that you're priced too high for the majority of your customers and too low for the ones that you're going to maximize profit
